meta.model: production value $ = $0.775M league min + $5.25M per WAR (fit through the minimum-salary floor) x stable multi-year-average WAR; surplus = production value - cap hit (positive = team-friendly bargain).
meta.honest: WAR is HISTORICAL (imported seasons) but the cap hit is the player's CURRENT contract -- a just-signed extension off a limited or down sample reads as an 'overpay' because the deal bets on a future the WAR cannot see. RAPM-based WAR also compresses elite defensemen and pure finishers. Single-season WAR is noisy; prefer >=2 seasons and read surplus as a range, not a point estimate.
